The final report and rolling write-up are the key map. Let me read both.

# Introspection Science — top-level write-up

Core question (whole project): **can a model distinguish a concept injected into its activations
from the same concept merely present in its prompt?** See `OVERALL_PLAN.md` for the full plan and
`proposal.md` for motivation. Work is organized into phases with phase-specific write-ups.

## ⭐ EXECUTIVE SUMMARY (the whole project, in brief) — see `final_report.md` for the standalone synthesis

**Answer: NO** — across three open models (Qwen2.5-32B/72B, Llama-3.3-70B), all injection layers (L0→L56),
and verbal-2AFC / first-token-latent / chain-of-thought readouts, the model shows **no source-attribution
introspection above the salience / anomaly / output-reading / prompt-inference floors**. The decisive test
(emphasize one concept in the prompt, inject the other at matched strength) has the model pick the
**injected** concept **below chance** in the primary order-pooled analysis at every layer × model
(per-model maxima 0.42 / 0.49 / 0.46; none survive BH-FDR + the CI-lower>0.55 bar; one non-significant
Llama near-orthogonal robustness cell reaches 0.55, CI spanning chance). This is a **powered null, not a
dead instrument**: the injection is demonstrably effective (aligned emphasize+inject → 0.70–0.80;
matched-norm random presence ~0.01–0.1 vs concept ~1.5–1.9), the readouts pass their positive controls
(0.84–1.00), and the salience/aligned arms are above chance. What the model CAN read is a weak, **prompt-dominated relative-internal-magnitude / anomaly** cue
that grows with depth — NOT source. An external linear probe (S8) sharpens this: prompt-vs-injection source
is **not decodable beyond re-reading-the-prompt + concept-agnostic graft/anomaly**, and in a balanced
two-concept design "which was injected" is **non-identifiable** from "which is more internally active"
(injection necessarily raises internal magnitude). The limiter looks **structural** (autoregressive
output-reading + off-manifold injection), not a capability deficit. **Carefully bounded:** NOT "models
cannot introspect" — it is a non-identifiability negative, specific to these models / injection methods /
readouts; 70–72B are below frontier/closed-Claude; linear/relational/off-manifold/by-construction bounds
stand.

**Deliverables (Segment 9):** `writeups/final_report.md` (the standalone paper-like synthesis — READ
THIS); `results/final_numbers_audit.md` (every headline number → committed source file + the
`--assert-cached` reproducibility verification); the consolidated figure set
`plots/final_fig{1,2,3,4}_*.png` (`plot_final.py`); `writeups/progress_log_s9_final_synthesis.md`.
Total project cost ≈ $1,066 (`total_cost.jsonl`). Phase takeaways for S0–S8 Phase 1 follow below.

## Phase `s8_phase1_symmetric_probe` (Segment 8, Phase 1 — the BALANCED two-concept source probe) — COMPLETE

**HEADLINE: the reviewer-recommended BALANCED two-concept 2AFC source probe gives a NON-IDENTIFIABILITY
NEGATIVE — "which co-present concept was injected" is not separable from "which is more internally active"
(relative magnitude), and no concept-general SOURCE tag is decodable beyond that.** Closes the single gap
Phase-0 flagged (GPT/Gemini's "cleanest source probe"). On `Qwen2.5-32B`, 20 cross-category pairs, held-out
5-fold by pair, readout {28,32,40,48,56,63}×{mean_ctx,last}. Grounds: **(1) Non-identifiability (load-
bearing, probe-independent):** the injected concept is ALWAYS the more-internally-active one (proj-z ~14σ
synthetic / ~4σ patch; the other co-present concept ~0 EVEN WHEN text-emphasized; mean|z_inj−z_oth|≈14σ,
frac<2σ≈0 — a salience-matched point is unconstructible), so a concept-conditioned MAGNITUDE model on
[z1,z2] decodes which was injected at 1.0 (ceiling) with NO residual headroom for a separate source signal;
an injection that does not raise internal activation is not an injection. **(2) Positive control + held-out
probe:** a held-out probe DETECTING injection-vs-none (non-relational) is 0.93–1.00 (the readout HAS power);
the held-out which-of-two SOURCE probe reaches 0.66 even in the cleanest output-presence-MATCHED decisive
arm (half-alpha symmetric) but stays a weak SHADOW of the concept-conditioned MAGNITUDE predictor
(proj-z oracle 0.92–1.0; source−oracle ≈ −0.3, does NOT beat the magnitude floor) and — output matched — is
specifically the INTERNAL proj-z bounded positive (S6§6C), NOT source (an MLP, 0.71, is also below the oracle). **(3) the
on-manifold patch cannot host the design** — a full-context all-or-nothing graft that ERASES the other
co-present concept (d output-presence→0.06, text-emphasis→0.00). **(4) Phase-0** is the powered
non-degenerate source test (negative: graft/anomaly, not source). The two salience axes dissociate as in
behavioral 5a (opposed-½: output-presence→emphasized, internal proj-z→injected). **Honesty note:** a
research-plan-reviewer caught that my initial "airtight" framing rested on two artifacts — the
salience-probe gap is DEFINITIONAL (injected≡higher-proj-z) and the opposed-arm 0.50 is DEGENERATE (identical
states under both labels) — so the verdict is re-grounded on the non-identifiability argument + the positive
control + Phase-0 + the patch erasure (see write-up "Corrections"). **Bounds:** non-identifiability
by-construction; LINEAR/single-MLP ≠ all probes; relational/attention-based source possible;
off-manifold/OOD. Details: `write_up_s8_phase1_symmetric_probe.md`; pre-reg `prereg_s8_phase1.md`
(pre-commit `1ef5076`); `progress_log_s8_phase1_symmetric_probe.md`; design review
`results/s8_phase1_design_review.md`. Plots: `plots/s8b_probe.png`, `plots/s8b_layers.png`. → **Segment-8
representational verdict = Phase-0 + Phase-1, both NEGATIVE. Segment 9 (synthesis) is next.**

## Phase `s8_mechanistic_probe` (Segment 8, Phase 0 — mechanistic: is source even linearly present?) — COMPLETE

**HEADLINE: no concept-general prompt-vs-injection SOURCE signal is linearly readable beyond the
re-reading-the-prompt cue + concept-agnostic anomaly (off-manifoldness / out-of-context-graft).** This closes
the project's repeated bound ("source might be present but not self-reported") in the no-decodable-source
direction, completing the behavioral negative at the representational level. On `Qwen2.5-32B`: **(A) external
linear source probe** — found + fixed a critical prompt-text confound (the instructions' asymmetric
route-P-vs-route-A design lets a probe trivially read "is the concept literally in the prompt" = re-reading the
prompt, saturating ALL floors at ~1.00; the matched-text fix was reviewed by 3 frontier models — Opus-4.7
endorsed, GPT-5.5 endorsed-with-an-estimand-caveat, Gemini-3 dissented; its anomaly-fallback objection is met by
the disjoint distractor + neutral graft floors). In the **matched-text** design (concept in the prompt for ALL
conditions; vary only the injection; held-out concepts; BOTH off-manifold-random + on-manifold-neutral-graft
floors + a strict DISJOINT distractor-concept-graft floor): synthetic ≈ random (off-manifold separability is
concept-AGNOSTIC); the probe is demonstrably NOT weak (it decodes concept content patch-vs-distractor ~0.97 +
off-manifoldness ~0.94 held-out); YET the prompt-vs-injection SOURCE boundary (prompt-vs-patch) is
concept-agnostic GRAFT — **transfer to neutral AND distractor grafts = 1.00**, Δ vs both floors = 0
(ceiling-saturated). **(B) The untried deny-direction ABLATION fails:** projecting the (valid) report/deny axis
out (narrow + broad, coeff≤2) is inert (concept_sub 0.00; no-injection FPR 0.00), while the additive form is a
yes-bias on a high FPR floor (bias 1.0: no-injection→1.00 ≥ concept) — replicating S3; neither lever recovers
concept-specific detection. **(C)** the model's "injection" verdict keys on prompt-inference (on-manifold
neutral graft → 1.00) + a WEAK manifold-status lean (synthetic 0.63 vs patch 0.54, concept-clustered CI
includes 0) — both non-source. **Bounds:** LINEAR probe ≠ all probes / ≠ "never accessible after training";
off-manifold/graft non-identifiability; matched-text estimand (additional injection on top of c-in-prompt).
Details: `write_up_s8_mechanistic_probe.md`; pre-reg `prereg_s8.md`; chronology
`progress_log_s8_mechanistic_probe.md`. Plots: `plots/s8_probe.png`, `plots/s8_deny.png`,
`plots/s8_anomaly.png`. → **Followed by Phase-1 (the balanced two-concept probe, above); together they are
the Segment-8 representational verdict.**

## Phase `s7_cross_model` (Segment 7 — cross-model generalization) — COMPLETE

**HEADLINE: the 32B source-attribution negative GENERALIZES across two contrasting models** —
`meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct` (**different family + more capable**) and `Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct`
(**within-family scale-up**). Repeating the decisive de-confounded battery on each (minimal library re-derived
on-model; hook re-verified 4/4; 2×H100 bf16): **no source discrimination above the salience/anomaly/output-
reading/prompt-inference floors at any coherent layer, on EITHER model.** The **decisive 5a opposed-emphasis
SOURCE test** picks the **injected** concept **< 0.5 at every layer×strength** (global max Llama **0.495**,
Qwen-72B **0.458**); **no source positive survives BH-FDR + the CI-lower>0.55 bar**; robust on near-orthogonal
pairs. The injection is **demonstrably EFFECTIVE** (not a failed injection): the **aligned** control
(emphasize+inject the same concept) gives picks-injected **0.70 (Llama) / 0.74 (Qwen-72B)** vs the emphasis
floor (0.432 / 0.021) and opposed (~0.45/~0.40), and a **matched-norm random** vector gives presence ~0.1 vs
the concept's ~1.5–1.9 — so the injection pulls the readout toward itself but **cannot be attributed to
source** (under opposed emphasis it only cancels the prompt to ≤chance, never overriding it). Same **non-source
structure** replicates: B1 relative-salience 2AFC ≈chance near input, **grows/peaks with depth** (Llama
single-peaked @L24 0.646 [0.52,0.77]; Qwen-72B monotone 0.48→0.61), collapses under opposed emphasis; B5
off-manifold synthetic→"injection" (latent 0.55–0.89) while the **on-manifold patch is read "prompt" despite
carrying full presence ~2.0** (Llama 0.17 @L8; Qwen-72B 0.19–0.35 through L40) → "injection" only with depth =
anomaly/manifold-status, not source. Light anchor: detection **output-presence-gated, sub≈0, FPR 0** with a
priming boost (Llama 0.156/0.094 = 1.66×; Qwen-72B 0.141/0.047 = 3.0×, matching 32B's 3.7×). §6C internal-z
dissociation null on both. **Injection-method caveats** (controlled, not source access): Llama garbles at a
lower perturbation (coherent presence-2 WORKING out-of-support → lead SUB latent) and its directions are less
orthogonal (abstract concepts collinear → re-run near-orthogonal pairs, holds); Qwen-72B is the cleanest
(orthogonal directions, comprehension 1.0, steers readily). **Verdict: the negative is NOT model-/family-specific
and is not explained by capability within the open-model range tested** — open models across two families and
the 32B→72B scale range, incl. a *more capable* model, all fail to track the injected source by this
(diff-of-means + verbal-2AFC/latent) method; with the injection demonstrably effective, this **supports the
structural limiter** (autoregressive output-reading + the off-manifold injection method) over a capability
explanation (bound: 70–72B is still below frontier/closed-Claude, so a frontier-capability positive is not
ruled out). On Llama the negative is carried by 5a+B1 (its B5 anomaly arm is thin, clean only at the early
L8/L16); Qwen-72B + 32B carry all three arms. CoT was not re-run cross-model (the weakest 32B channel).
Segment 8 (mechanism) is the stretch; else
Segment 9 (synthesis). Details: `write_up_s7_cross_model.md`; pre-reg `prereg_s7.md`; chronology
`progress_log_s7_cross_model.md`. Plots: `plots/s7_headline_llama70b.png`, `plots/s7_headline_qwen72b.png`.

## Phase `s6_layer_sweep` (Segment 6 — injection-layer sweep + the bounded-positive dissociation) — COMPLETE

**HEADLINE: the S5 source-attribution negative is LAYER-INVARIANT.** Sweeping the injection layer
(L0–L56, 10 layers) for the de-confounded battery on 32B, **no injection layer yields source
discrimination that overrides prompt salience** (5a opposed picks-injected **<0.5 at every layer**, global
max 0.42 @L24; the model follows the ~1σ prompt-emphasized concept over the 20–50σ injection; **none survive
BH-FDR**; replicated on the 46-concept full set at L8/L40/L48). A real but weak injection **PULL** emerges
with depth (+0.21 [0.10,0.33] above the emphasis floor @L40) but never overrides the prompt, and the
opposed test can't separate weak-source from salience. The divergence the model *can* read is **relative
salience + anomaly that GROW WITH DEPTH** — the proposal's geometry, reinterpreted as salience/anomaly,
NOT source: the relative-salience 2AFC is **≈chance near the input** (L0–8), **single-peaked at L40** (sub
latent **0.742**; quadratic-shape perm p=0.05), then falls — and the **held-out 46-concept full set
confirms the L40 peak is layer-localized** (0.707 [0.63,0.78] @L40 vs ≈chance @L8/L48); the on-manifold
patch flips from "prompt" to **"injection" at L40** (held-out 0.996) = a sharp anomaly onset (the
late-layer L48/L56 flip is patch-presence-collapse, not anomaly). A direct **representational-geometry**
measurement shows the premise itself does not cleanly hold: cos(injection-state, prompt-state) is ~0.30 at
the matched L24 site (max 0.34 mid-stack), **barely above the cross-concept floor 0.26** (not
concept-specifically aligned), and the injection is far
off the natural manifold (z 2.6–6.3σ vs prompt ~0.4σ) — early injection is *ineffective*, not
prompt-equivalent. **Bounded-positive verdict (S5-deferred, partially resolved):** the controlled-2AFC
bounded positive's readout tracks the injected concept's **internal projection-z, NOT realized
output-presence** (within-layer β_z +0.078 [0.001,0.169], survives partialling out impending-output;
β_presence −0.05) — but it is **weak, the comparative β_z>β_presence is NOT significant, the between-layer
association is opposite, the z is off-manifold anomaly-magnitude, and it is dominated by prompt-text**
⇒ weak within-layer internal-magnitude sensitivity, **NOT a clean introspection positive, NOT source.**
**CoT recovers no source** (says "injection" at the same 0.6–0.98 for a real injection, a content-free
RANDOM vector, and NO injection alike — prompt-inference; CoT-2AFC ≈ the first-token latent). Comprehension
1.00. **Stay on 32B; Segment 7 (cross-model) is the key external-validity check** — carry the mid-late
salience/anomaly band + the §6C dissociation + the 5a opposed test. Details:
`write_up_s6_layer_sweep.md`; pre-reg `prereg_s6.md`; chronology `progress_log_s6_layer_sweep.md`. Plots:
`plots/s6_curves.png`, `s6_geometry.png`, `s6_dissociation_cot.png`, `match_layers.png`.

## Phase `s5_discrimination_full` (Segment 5 — full-scale discrimination + the headline answer) — COMPLETE

**HEADLINE ANSWER to the project's core question: NO reliable source discrimination above the
salience/anomaly/inference floors** — Qwen2.5-32B does not distinguish a concept *injected into its
activations* from the same concept *present in its prompt* as a source, under the methods/layer/verbal-
2AFC readouts tested (carefully bounded: a weak internal cue exists but is dominated by prompt-primacy /
semantic-anomaly / textual inference, and the cleanest on-manifold comparison is partly non-identifiable
— NOT "32B has zero source information"). At full scale (46 robust concepts, 23 cross-category pairs,
L24, first-token latent + sampled, concept-clustered CIs) every apparent discrimination is a confound:
(5a) the controlled which-of-two 2AFC is only weakly above chance sub-threshold (latent **0.59**
[0.505,0.672]) and is **dominated by prompt-text salience** — under opposed prompt-emphasis the model
picks the **injected** concept only **0.39** (< the 0.61–0.78 prompt-emphasis floor). A per-trial
verification shows the injected concept actually **dominates the output (presence 2.78) and internal
state (~46σ)** while the prompt-emphasized one is suppressed (0.97) — yet the model picks the prompt-
emphasized one, so at working strength the readout is **neither a simple output-presence nor a simple
internal-magnitude maximizer, and not source** (weak baseline-subtracted injection pull only +0.17
[0.095,0.242]): it follows the prompt, not the source; (5b) the
explicit "prompt or injection?" calls the off-manifold synthetic **"injection" 0.95** but the (position-
for-position) **on-manifold patch "prompt"** (says-inj 0.45) despite the patch having **5× higher
presence** (1.95 vs 0.36) — manifold/anomaly detection, not source, not presence-driven; (5c) yet the
2AFC reads that *same* patch as injected (0.73) — an internal inconsistency proving it is salience;
(prompt-inference) the positive-control source question is solved by "X absent from my text ⇒ injection"
(paraphrase 0.996; explicit-source says "injection" at *every* layer in the §7 cell). Instrument valid
(positive-control K5 PASS prompt-lit 1.00 / inject-sub 0.89; comprehension 1.00). **Plus a bounded
relative-salience positive — a weak salience cue dominated by prompt-text, not internal-magnitude
introspection:** 32B reads which co-present concept is internally more active for affective/object
concepts (emotion[8] 0.74, object[7] 0.67; concrete/abstract ≈ chance; positive[2]/persona[2] ≤2 concepts;
**style[3] inverted 0.19 = signal**). A **per-trial verification** (`run_s5_match_verify`, 6-concept
subset) shows the §6 opposed working point is **not presence-matched**: the injected concept *dominates*
the output (presence **2.78**, gap CI excludes 0) and internal state (~46σ) while the prompt-emphasized
concept is suppressed (**0.97**) — **yet the model picks the prompt-emphasized (less-emitted) concept
0.61**, so the readout follows **prompt-text salience**, and is neither a simple output-presence nor a
simple internal-magnitude maximizer, not source. The weak injection pull (diff-in-diff +0.17
[0.095,0.242]) is a residual sensitivity not yet separable into output vs faint internal cue (deferred to
S6/8) ⇒ a weak salience cue dominated by the prompt, **not source-attribution.** **All S4-flagged refinements implemented** (matched salience EXERCISED to common presence+
coherence points; **position-for-position on-manifold patch** — now reaches a *coherent output-present
on-manifold state*, presence 1.95, treated as prompt; counterbalanced diff-in-diff; per-concept held-out
+ category breakouts; comprehension + coherence gates). **Bounds:** the negative is "no source-attribution
above the salience/anomaly/output-reading/prompt-inference floors under the methods+protocols tested on
**32B**" — a coherent output-present on-manifold concept state is constructible but reads as prompt (on-
manifold concept content grafted into context *is* prompt-like, by construction); not a claim that 32B
fundamentally cannot introspect on source. → Segment 6 (layer sweep), Segment 7 (cross-model) are the
follow-ups. Details: `write_up_s5_discrimination_full.md`; pre-reg `prereg_s5.md`; chronology
`progress_log_s5_discrimination_full.md`. Plots: `plots/s5_headline.png`, `s5_extra.png`,
`matched_points.png`.

## Phase `s4_benchmark_design_pilot` (Segment 4 — design + pilot the discrimination benchmark) — COMPLETE

**Key takeaway:** (A) the **output-decoupling test** converts S3's "consistent with output-reading"
into a clean experimental result — holding internal magnitude fixed at working and forcing the
concept's output appearance low (token suppression), clear-detection collapses to **0** where realized
presence is actually low (0/14 at presence≤1; within coherent `pirate`, detection 1.00→0.50 as
presence drops) → **detection is gated by realized output-presence, not internal magnitude**; no
output-independent introspection on 32B. (B) the **prompt-vs-activation source-attribution benchmark is
designed, reviewed, pre-registered, and piloted** (`writeups/benchmark_design_s4.md`): controlled 2AFC
("which of two co-present concepts is injected", neutral digit labels) + positive-control 2AFC +
first-token latent (output-reading-severed) readout; matched salience load-bearing on output-presence;
MANDATORY content-free + on-manifold-patch anomaly controls; trivial-prompt-reading control;
pre-registered kill criterion K1–K5. **Pilot outcome = K2 (anomaly) + K3 (salience/prompt-inference):
NOT source-attribution introspection — and the design's own controls proved it.** The instrument is
valid for format/knowable-source (positive-control + comprehension at ceiling); the controlled 2AFC is
above chance sub-threshold (latent **0.77**, ΔP +0.44 — a genuine readout of which co-present concept
is internally more active) **but** three de-confounded controls show it is salience, not source: (FU1,
counterbalanced) under **opposed** prompt-emphasis the model picks the injected concept only **0.10**;
(FU2, scope-matched) the *explicit* "prompt or injection?" question attributes the off-manifold synthetic
to "injection" (1.00, both scopes) but the on-manifold patch to "prompt" (~0.55); (FU3) yet the 2AFC
discriminates that same patch even better (0.94) — labelling it "injected" while the model calls it
"prompt". The pilot found **no instrument that isolates source from salience/anomaly/prompt-dominance**.
A clean, fair negative; **stay on 32B**. Details:
`write_up_s4_benchmark_design_pilot.md`; design doc `benchmark_design_s4.md`; chronology
`progress_log_s4_benchmark_design_pilot.md`. Plots: `plots/decouple_output_independence.png`,
`discrim_pilot.png`, `discrim_followup.png`.

## Phase `s3_confounds_validity` (Segment 3 — confound controls, validity & the gate) — COMPLETE

**Key takeaway:** the introspection "detection" on Qwen2.5-32B is **concept-specific but
output-dependent** — the pre-registered replication gate (`results/replication_gate.md`, committed
before any confound result) returns **PARTIAL**. Established with a multi-axis-matched content-free
perturbation battery + identification specificity + output-independence/timing + extended latent
battery + forced-choice + a detection-bias-vector attempt (all reuse the validated S2 graders/protocol;
no `steering_modal.py` edits, so S2 caches preserved). Primed, L24, det==2, n=96/cell, Wilson CIs:
- **Concept-specific, NOT generic anomaly detection (gate Component 1 PASS):** concept@working detection
  **0.208 [0.139,0.300]** vs a content-free vector at **matched projection-z** (50.8σ, coherent): random
  **0.000**, shuffled **0.010** — holds at matched coherence and matched raw norm too. A random vector
  equally/more off-manifold yet coherent is essentially never reported as injected. (Key nuance:
  projection-z ≠ functional impact — a 50σ random vector leaves output coherent/unbiased.)
- **Identification-specific (Component 2 PASS):** P(correct cluster id | named)=0.75 vs ~0 content-free
  confabulation.
- **Output-READING, not output-independent (Component 3 FAIL):** detection ∝ output-presence (0.00 at
  presence 0 → 0.34 at presence 3); at matched-low/sub-threshold presence concept detection = perturbation
  = FPR ≈ **0.000**, behaviorally, latently (P(Yes)≈0), and under forced-choice. **Stronger elicitation
  tried and does not recover sub signal:** forced-choice 2AFC diff-in-diff +0.026 [-0.097,0.149] (spans
  0); a detection-bias vector raises detection EQUALLY for concept/random/no-injection (→~1.0) = generic
  yes-bias.
- **Decision (→ Segment 4):** the discrimination benchmark proceeds on 32B (do not escalate model first
  — the limiter is the structural autoregressive output-reading confound, not under-trying), but the
  **content-free perturbation baseline + naturalistic/on-manifold (activation-patched) injection become
  MANDATORY**, and **matching on downstream output-presence is load-bearing** (internal-axis matching
  alone is insufficient). Details: `write_up_s3_confounds_validity.md`; chronology
  `progress_log_s3_confounds_validity.md`; gate `results/replication_gate.md`. Plots: `plots/perturb_*.png`,
  `timing_output_independence.png`, `forced_choice.png`, `refusal_bias.png`.

## Phase `s2_introspection_replication` (Segment 2 — replicate introspection) — COMPLETE

**Key takeaway:** the concept-injection introspection result **partially replicates on
`Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct`, and is honestly weak + confounded** — established rigorously (validated
detection + identification graders, 0% no-injection FPR, n=460/cell, a logit-lens readout, a
collider-free sub-threshold check, and a matched-calibration confirmation). Headline (stage-2, 46
robust concepts, all-positions; primary metric = CLEAR detection, grader score 2): at the **genuinely
sub-threshold** operating point detection ≈ **0** (0/460 at L24; on the subset only **0.34%** of
output-unbiased cells flag a detection, and just **12%** of those name the right concept — e.g. inject
fear→"snake", cat→"laser": detection-without-identification at a tiny rate). At **working** strength
clear detection = **0.104 [0.080,0.136]** (det≥1 0.133), vs plain 0.028 — **priming ~3.7× at zero FPR**
(matched-calibration L32 confirms: 0.078). **detection > identification** as a conditional: given a
clear detection it names correctly ~**73%** of the time, but detection itself is rare. **Early layers
NOT easier** (inverted-U, mid-stack L16–24 best, L8≈0; not perturbation-matched). **Concept-type
dominates:** persona/emotion/abstract ≫ concrete-noun ~0.01–0.07 (caveat: persona/positive thin, 1–2
concepts). **Position scope decisive:** context-only "residue" ⇒ ~0 detection (and little downstream
trace). **Logit-lens:** detection-answer P(Yes) → ~0.33 at working, latent>verbalized (~27% of
greedy-"No" cells P(Yes)>0.2), but ≈0 sub-threshold/context-only; concept-token elevation is
mechanical. **Implication for Seg 3:** measurable introspection lives only where output is
concurrently biased (confounded), and is elicitation/method/model-specific; informs the replication
gate / possible model reconsideration. Details: `write_up_s2_introspection_replication.md`;
chronology `progress_log_s2_introspection_replication.md`. Plots: `plots/intro_*.png`,
`plots/logitlens_*.png`.

## Phase `s1_concept_library` (Segment 1 — main-model concept library) — COMPLETE

**Key takeaway:** the concept-vector **library is built and validated on the MAIN model
`Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct`** (one H100 80 GB). Hook re-verified (9 layers, no off-by-one);
per-layer norms re-measured (first-token sink persists → median content-token norm used). A
**diverse 51-concept dataset** (47 non-style across 7 categories + 4 styles, near-neighbor pairs, ~40 vivid
length-matched contrastive pairs each; the key fix was **vivid, varied, length-matched neutrals** so
diff-of-means is concept-specific, not valence — the affective neutrals are mood-balanced so the
cosine check passes (neg-emotion max cos 0.51, fear↔joy 0.03). Hardened extraction adds train/test-
split, bootstrap-stability, and a shuffled-label negative control. **50/51 concepts steer cleanly**
by a per-generation joint criterion (presence≥2 AND coherence≥2), **confirmed on held-out prompts**
(held-out joint-pass mean 0.95): nouns 25/25, abstract 11/12, emotion 10/10, style 4/4 incl. all_caps
which failed on 7B; **secrecy** the lone non-clean concept (degenerate at strength). Tiered honestly as **46 robust / 4 borderline / 1 fail (secrecy)**. Beats no-injection (~0) and
matched-norm random (~0), with a **near-neighbor specificity matrix** that is BIMODAL (non-emotion
near-neighbors cleanly discriminable ~0.50; emotion near-neighbors NOT, ~1.09 — flagged in the
library; distant foils 0.11). Strength is
reported in **multiple units** (alpha_rel ~1.0; projection-z ~39σ off-manifold; post-RMSNorm ~0.73)
with behavioral + sub-threshold calibration. The grader was **re-validated on 32B** (hand-label 18/18;
GPT-5.5 + Gemini both 100% within-1 of Opus). Packaged as `results/concept_library.json` (manifest) +
`results/concept_library.npz` (committed float16 vectors). An early introspection read on 32B shows
output-drift but near-zero genuine sub-threshold detection (1/44), no yes-bias, priming unhelpful —
modestly more encouraging than 7B but small-sample (flagged for Segment 2).

**Details:** `write_up_s1_concept_library.md` (full methodology/choices/schema/caveats),
`progress_log_s1_concept_library.md` (chronology), `continuation_context.md` (how to run).
**Plots:** `plots/steering_effectiveness.png`, `plots/specificity_matrix.png`,
`plots/steering_per_concept.png`, `plots/extraction_stability.png`, `plots/concept_cosine_matrix.png`,
`plots/introspection_smoke.png`. **Library:** `results/concept_library.json` + `concept_library.npz`.

## Phase `s0_infra_derisk` (Segment 0 — infra + steering de-risk) — COMPLETE

**Key takeaway:** the activation-steering infrastructure is **working and validated**. On
Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (Modal H100 + HF forward-hooks), difference-of-means concept vectors injected
into the residual stream **reliably steer generation toward the concept for 5/6 de-risk concepts**
(cat/ocean/fire/volcano/bridge), LLM-judge-graded, **beating no-injection and a matched-norm
random-vector control** (the random control is *live* — its coherence collapses too — but never
produces the concept), with a clean specificity matrix and an identified usable window
(`alpha_rel ≈ 1–1.5` × the median residual norm; coherence collapses above ~2). The hook is
**provably non-trivial** (α=0 no-op, α≠0 changes logits, extraction/injection layer-aligned with
no off-by-one, fires on every decode step). The grader is validated (16/16 vs hand labels; within-1
agreement with GPT-5.5 and Gemini on real outputs). A cheap introspection smoke test gives the
early read that, on 7B, injection causes **output-drift** toward the concept while the model
**denies** detecting an injected thought (zero false-positives on controls; **0 explicit detections
in 24 genuinely sub-threshold trials**; the single genuine-ish detection across 60 trials — fire@L8,
output *not* fire-themed, "intense and fierce" — is at an early layer, the literature's predicted
signature) — i.e. the effect is **weak on 7B**, motivating scale-up. A layer scan shows very-early
layers 0–2 inject the concept at low strength but with coherence~0 (the "layer-0 ≈ prompt route"
regime); coherent steering is mid-stack (8–14); late 20/24 need more strength.

The stylistic concept `all_caps` does **not** steer output (a constant difference-of-means
residual vector doesn't implement the token-level caps policy at tested layers) — documented as an
expected limitation with follow-ups.

**Details:** see `write_up_s0_infra_derisk.md` (full methodology, choices, plots, caveats),
`progress_log_s0_infra_derisk.md` (chronology), `continuation_context.md` (how to run / gotchas).

**Plots:** `plots/steering_effectiveness.png`, `plots/specificity_matrix.png`,
`plots/steering_per_concept.png`, `plots/layer_scan.png`, `plots/introspection_smoke.png`.

**Cost so far:** $16.05 (Modal GPU $2.24 + Opus $13.06 + ~$0.75 estimated untracked cross-check/reviews).

**Next (after Segment 1):** Segment 2 — replicate introspection (detection + identification) on the
32B model using the validated library, emphasizing the sub-threshold regime + the
introspection-priming/forced-choice/logit-lens protocol.

content.thinking: I'm looking at the final report to understand the overall findings, since this project ended up being a negative result.

content.text: This is comprehensive. Now the standalone final report, which is the key synthesis.

# Can a language model introspect on the *source* of its own internal state? A fair, pre-registered, cross-model negative — and the methodology that makes it trustworthy

*Final synthesis report (Segment 9). Standalone — readable without the phase logs. Model family under
study: open-weight instruction models (Qwen2.5-32B / 72B, Llama-3.3-70B). All headline numbers trace to a
committed `results/*.json` (see `results/final_numbers_audit.md`).*

---

## Abstract

We ask a sharp, falsifiable introspection question: **can a model distinguish a concept that has been
*injected into its activations* from the same concept merely being *present in its prompt*?** A reliable,
fair, above-chance discrimination of the **source** of an internal state — not explained by salience
mismatch, generic off-manifold/anomaly detection, the model reading its own already-biased output, or
prompt-inference — would be evidence of genuine introspective access to the source/nature of an internal
state. Across a nine-segment, pre-registered program — a validated steering pipeline, a 46-concept vector
library, a replication of the introspection effect, a de-confounding gate, a purpose-built discrimination
benchmark, a full-scale run, an injection-layer sweep (L0→L56), a cross-model generalization (Qwen2.5-32B
/ 72B, Llama-3.3-70B), and an external-probe representational analysis — **the answer is no: the model
shows no source-attribution above the salience / anomaly / output-reading / prompt-inference floors**,
under the injection methods (diff-of-means + position-for-position on-manifold sequence patch), layers,
readouts (verbal-2AFC / first-token-latent / chain-of-thought), and models tested. The decisive test —
emphasize one concept in the prompt and inject the *other* at matched strength — has the model pick the
**injected** concept **below chance, in the primary order-pooled analysis, at every** layer × model
(per-model maxima 0.42 / 0.49 / 0.46; none survive BH-FDR + a pre-registered CI-lower > 0.55 bar; the
strongest secondary signal anywhere is the Llama near-orthogonal-pair robustness re-run, which peaks at 0.55 (L24),
with a CI spanning chance — §5/§9). The result is **a genuine, powered null, not a dead instrument**: the
injection is demonstrably effective (aligning it with the prompt moves the readout to 0.70–0.80; a
matched-norm random vector carries output-presence ~0.01–0.1 vs the concept's ~1.5–1.9), the readouts pass
their positive controls (0.84–1.00),
and the aligned/salience arms are above chance. What the model *can* read is a **weak, prompt-dominated,
relative-internal-magnitude / anomaly cue that grows with depth** — *not* source. An external linear probe
sharpens this at the representational level: prompt-vs-injection source is **not decodable beyond
re-reading-the-prompt + a concept-agnostic graft/anomaly signature**, and in a balanced two-concept design
"which concept was injected" is **non-identifiable** from "which concept is more internally active"
(injection necessarily raises internal magnitude). We interpret the limiter as **structural** (the
autoregressive residual stream partly *is* the impending output; the diff-of-means injection is
off-manifold), not a 32B-specific capability deficit — a more-capable model and a within-family scale-up
both fail. The contribution is two-fold: (1) a rigorous, fair, cross-model **negative** answer to a
well-posed introspection question, and (2) a reusable **methodology** for separating genuine
source-attribution from its confounds. We state the negative at calibrated strength: strong (clean, fair,
pre-registered, cross-model, layer-invariant, behaviorally + representationally converging) **and**
bounded (model/method/readout-specific; 70–72B below frontier/closed-Claude; linear/relational/
off-manifold/by-construction non-identifiability) — **not** a metaphysical "models cannot introspect."

---

## 1. The question and why it matters

There are two ways to put the concept "cat" into a model's processing:

- **Prompt route:** add "cat" tokens to the input text.
- **Activation route:** add a "cat" steering vector (a difference-of-means direction) into the residual
  stream at some layer *L*.

If you inject the vector at the very first layer, the two routes are nearly identical. The interesting
scientific question (from the project `proposal.md` / `OVERALL_PLAN.md`) is **how they diverge when the
vector is injected at *later* layers, and whether the model can tell the difference.** If a model can
reliably report "this concept is in my activations because something was injected" vs "this concept is in
my input because I read it," that is evidence of **introspective access to the source/nature of an
internal state** — not merely re-reading the prompt. If it cannot, then much of the apparent
"introspection" in the literature may reduce to steering-altered prompt interpretation.

The hard part is that a naïve "above-chance discrimination" can be produced by **four confounds**, none of
which is genuine source-introspection. The entire program is built to defeat them:

1. **Salience mismatch.** A strong injection vs a faint prompt mention (or vice versa) is trivially
   discriminable on *intensity*, not source.
2. **Anomaly / off-manifold detection.** A static, position-agnostic diff-of-means vector is *detectably
   weird* — the model may notice "my activations are off-distribution" rather than attribute a concept's
   source. (A prompt-borne concept is contextualized and on-manifold.)
3. **Output-reading.** In an autoregressive model the residual stream partly *is* the impending output, so
   "reading internal state" and "reading my own about-to-be-emitted, concept-biased tokens" are not
   cleanly separable. At output-biasing strength "detection" can be trivial.
4. **Prompt-inference.** "The concept X is active but I never read X ⇒ it must have been injected" is
   *inference about the input*, not direct introspective access (the "Dissociating Direct Access from
   Inference" concern).

A positive result only counts as introspection if it survives **all four**. Defeating them — fairly,
quantitatively, and reproducibly — is half the contribution.

---

## 2. The headline answer (carefully bounded)

**No source-attribution introspection above the salience / anomaly / output-reading / prompt-inference
floors**, under the injection methods (diff-of-means + position-for-position on-manifold sequence patch),
the layers (L0→L56), the readouts (verbal-2AFC / first-token-latent / chain-of-thought), and the models
(Qwen2.5-32B, Qwen2.5-72B, Llama-3.3-70B) tested — **plus** a precisely-bounded *relative-salience*
positive that is **not** source-attribution.

The single decisive test is **5a opposed-emphasis**: the prompt emphasizes concept B while concept A is
injected at matched strength; the model is asked (in a neutral digit-labelled 2AFC, read at the **first
answer token** so the decision is severed from any concept-biased output) which concept is injected. If it
tracks source, it picks the **injected** concept (A) above chance; if it follows salience, it picks the
prompt-emphasized one (B). In the primary order-pooled analysis the model picks the **injected** concept
**below chance at every layer × model** (Figure 1a) — the below-chance value is **systematic
prompt-following** (it reliably follows the ~1σ prompt over the 20–50σ injection), not noisy guessing.
(The sampled verbal-2AFC and CoT readouts are convergent but less decisive; the one secondary-analysis
exception, on Llama, is non-significant — see §5 “S7” and §9.) Per-model maxima:

| Model | global max P(picks injected), opposed | layer of max | survives the source criterion? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Qwen2.5-32B | **0.42** | L24 | no (BH-FDR over 20 tests) |
| Llama-3.3-70B | **0.495** (primary) / 0.55 n.s. (near-orthogonal robustness re-run) | L24 | no (BH-FDR + CI-lower>0.55, 12 tests) |
| Qwen2.5-72B | **0.458** | L40 | no (BH-FDR + CI-lower>0.55, 14 tests) |

(The CI-lower>0.55 leg was pre-registered for the S7 cross-model runs; on 32B [S5/S6] the pre-registered
criterion is BH-FDR alone, which is moot since the max is 0.42. On Llama, dropping its shared-subspace
concept pairs and re-running on the near-orthogonal pairs lifts the L24 opposed point estimate to **0.55**
but with a CI **[0.39, 0.72]** spanning chance — it does **not** clear the 0.55 bar; see §5/§9.)

This is **not** a failed injection (Figure 1b). When the injection is *aligned* with the prompt
(emphasize + inject the **same** concept), picks-injected rises to **0.73 (32B) / 0.70 (Llama) / 0.74
(Qwen-72B)** — the injection clearly moves the readout. A matched-norm **random** vector carries
output-presence ~0.01–0.1 vs the concept's ~1.5–1.9. So the injection pulls the readout *toward itself* when
not opposed; it simply **cannot be attributed to source**: under opposed emphasis it only cancels the
prompt toward chance, never overriding it.

**What this is NOT.** It is **not** the metaphysical claim "models cannot introspect on the source of a
state." It is a **bounded, non-identifiability** negative: a weak internal cue exists but is swamped by
the prompt, the cleanest on-manifold injected state is *prompt-like by construction*, and "which is
injected" is non-identifiable from "which is more internally active" (§6, §8, §9).

![Figure 1 — headline cross-model 5a opposed-emphasis source test + injection-effectiveness](../plots/final_fig1_headline_crossmodel.png)

---

## 3. Methodology / validity scaffolding (half the contribution)

The negative is only worth anything because the instrument is **fair** and **validated**. The reusable
methodology:

- **A validated steering pipeline + concept-vector library.** Difference-of-means concept vectors injected
  into the residual stream via forward-hooks (Modal GPU). Built and validated on the *main* model
  (Qwen2.5-32B): 51 concepts across 7+ categories with **vivid, length-matched, mood-balanced** contrastive
  pairs (so the direction is concept-specific, not valence); train/test split + bootstrap stability +
  shuffled-label control; **50/51 steer cleanly**, tiered honestly as **46 robust / 4 borderline / 1 fail**,
  held-out joint-pass mean 0.95. Strength reported in multiple units (alpha_rel ~1.0, projection-z ~39σ
  off-manifold, post-RMSNorm ~0.73) with a behavioral coherence calibration.
- **The discrimination benchmark.** A **controlled which-of-two 2AFC** ("both A and B are in the prompt;
  one is *also* injected — which?") with **neutral digit labels** (never the concept word — injecting A's
  vector mechanically boosts the "A" token, so a word answer would be a direct-logit artifact),
  counterbalanced over option order and which-is-injected; a **positive-control 2AFC** (knowable source,
  for instrument validation); the **5a opposed-emphasis** source test; and an **explicit
  prompt-or-injection** readout.
- **Multi-axis matched salience, load-bearing on OUTPUT-PRESENCE.** Concept "strength" can be matched on
  several axes (downstream output concept-presence; logit lift; internal projection-z). S3 proved
  *projection-z ≠ functional impact* (a 50σ random vector is coherent and undetected), so matching is
  **exercised by the strength knob to common output-presence operating points** — not just characterized,
  and not by selecting trials on realized presence (a collider).
- **Mandatory anomaly controls.** A **content-free perturbation baseline** (random/shuffled vector at
  matched axis) and a **naturalistic / on-manifold activation-patch** condition — real concept-bearing
  residuals grafted **position-for-position** onto the context, reaching a *coherent, output-present,
  on-manifold* state (presence ~1.95). If discrimination works only for the off-manifold synthetic and
  collapses for the on-manifold patch, the benchmark measures anomaly, not source.
- **Prompt-inference / paraphrase control.** A paraphrase variant (concept present semantically, the
  literal word absent) measures literal vs semantic re-reading; the symmetric two-concept frame neutralizes
  the literal route by construction.
- **Instrument validation via positive controls + comprehension.** A "which concept did the prompt
  emphasize more?" comprehension check (no injection) and a knowable-source positive control confirm the
  model understands the 2AFC frame and the digit channel before any null is interpreted.
- **First-token-latent output-severing.** The source decision is read at the **first answer token**, before
  any concept-biased token is emitted (logit-lens over the two digit/concept options) — the cleanest
  available attack on the output-reading confound. Reported alongside the sampled channel; they agree.
- **Pre-registration + outcome-licensing + MC-aware criteria.** Each segment's plan, kill criterion, and
  the outcome-licensing legs (i)–(v) were committed **before** results (git hashes in the progress logs);
  concept-clustered bootstrap CIs; BH-FDR over the layer × arm family; a pre-registered CI-lower > 0.55
  source bar.

---

## 4. Falsifiability & power — what a *positive* would have looked like, and why the null is genuine

This is a **powered null, not an unfalsifiable one.**

**What a positive result would have required** (the pre-registered outcome-licensing, design-doc §6, legs
i–v): a controlled-2AFC / probe signal that **(i)** survives output-presence + coherence matching, **(ii)**
exceeds the content-free perturbation baseline, **(iii)** holds for the **on-manifold patch** in the
*source* sense, **(iv)** appears **sub-threshold**, and **(v)** is **not** explained by prompt-inference
(survives paraphrase, exceeds the positive-control prompt-reading ceiling) — AND clears the CI-lower > 0.55
/ MC-aware bar. Concretely, the 5a opposed test would have to pick the **injected** concept **> 0.5**
(ideally > 0.55) at some coherent layer on some model. It never does.

**The instruments had the power to detect such a positive** (Figure 3a):

- The injection is **demonstrably effective**: aligned emphasize+inject ≈ 0.70–0.80 (up to 0.97 at the
  deepest Qwen-72B layer); matched-norm random output-presence ~0.01–0.1 vs concept ~1.5–1.9.
- The readouts **pass their positive controls**: S5 knowable-source positive control = prompt-literal
  **1.00**, inject-sub **0.89** (the latent positive control is weaker at the *working* strength, 0.63 —
  the K1 output-presence cost — which is why we lead the source test on the *sub* latent + the matched
  point); comprehension **1.00**; the S8 probe reads concept content (patch-vs-neutral **~0.99** held-out)
  and detects injection-vs-none (**0.89–1.00** held-out across the Phase-0 route classifier and the
  Phase-1 detect-injection positive control).
- The salience / aligned arms **are above chance** (the relative-salience 2AFC peaks per-model at
  **0.61–0.74**; aligned 0.70–0.80).

So the **source-specific** null sits *inside* an instrument that is otherwise lively: a real absence of
source signal, not a dead probe.

![Figure 3 — falsifiability & power: instruments pass their positive controls; detection is output-presence-gated](../plots/final_fig3_falsifiability_power.png)

---

## 5. The results arc (S0 → S8)

A coherent through-line; key numbers with CIs. (Per-segment detail in `writeups/write_up_s*.md`.)

- **S0 — infra de-risk (Qwen2.5-7B).** Difference-of-means steering works and is provably non-trivial
  (α=0 no-op; layer-aligned; fires every decode step), beating no-injection + matched-norm random with a
  clean specificity matrix. An early smoke test shows weak/absent genuine sub-threshold detection on 7B →
  scale up.
- **S1 — the 32B concept library.** 51 concepts; **50/51 steer cleanly** (46 robust); held-out joint-pass
  0.95; projection-z ~39σ; a documented near-neighbor specificity caveat (emotion near-neighbors are not
  finely discriminable). Packaged as `results/concept_library.{json,npz}`.
- **S2 — replication (weak + output-confounded).** The introspection effect **partially replicates** on
  32B but is honestly weak: at the genuinely sub-threshold operating point, clear detection ≈ **0**
  (0/460); the only measurable signal is at **working** strength where the output is concurrently
  concept-biased — clear detection **0.104** primed vs **0.028** plain (**priming ~3.7×** on clear
  detection; ~2.6× on the inclusive det≥1 metric; at 0 FPR, 0/120). Detection > identification only as a
  conditional (P(correct id | clear detect) **0.73**). The
  deep caveat: the residual stream partly *is* the impending output.
- **S3 — the pre-registered de-confounding gate = PARTIAL.** Working-strength detection is
  **concept/subspace-specific** (refutes generic raw-off-manifoldness anomaly detection: a content-free
  vector at **matched projection-z = 50.8σ** gives random **0.000** / shuffled **0.010** vs concept
  **0.208 [0.139, 0.300]**, answers staying coherent) **and identification-specific** (P(cluster-correct |
  named) **0.75**) — **but entirely output-presence-gated** (detection rises 0.00 → 0.34 with output
  presence; at sub-threshold / matched-low presence, concept detection = perturbation = FPR ≈ **0**
  behaviorally, latently, and under forced-choice). **No output-independent introspection on 32B.**
  Decision: the content-free + on-manifold controls become **mandatory** and salience matching must be
  **load-bearing on output-presence**.
- **S4 — benchmark design + pilot → kill criterion triggered (K2 anomaly + K3 salience/prompt-inference).**
  The controlled 2AFC is above chance sub-threshold (pilot latent **0.77**) — a genuine readout of *which
  co-present concept is internally more active* — **but** three de-confounded controls show it is **not**
  source: under opposed emphasis it picks the injected concept only **0.10** (FU1, prompt-dominated); the
  *explicit* question calls the off-manifold synthetic "injection" (**1.00**) but the on-manifold patch
  "prompt" (~0.55) (FU2, anomaly-gated); yet the 2AFC discriminates that *same* patch even better
  (**0.94**) while the explicit question calls it "prompt" (FU3, an internal inconsistency). No instrument
  isolates source from salience/anomaly/prompt-dominance.
- **S5 — full-scale negative (the headline).** 46 robust concepts, 23 cross-category pairs, L24, latent +
  sampled, concept-clustered CIs. 5a opposed picks-injected **0.39 [0.34, 0.44]** (latent; clustered
  [0.33, 0.45]) at matched
  working strength — and a **per-trial verification** showed the routes are *not* presence-matched in the
  competition: the **injected** concept actually *dominates* the output (presence **2.78**, gap CI excludes
  0) and internal state (~46σ) while the prompt-emphasized one is suppressed (**0.97**) — **yet the model
  picks the prompt-emphasized concept 0.61**. So the readout follows **prompt text**, is neither an
  output-presence nor an internal-magnitude maximizer, and is not source. The explicit channel:
  synthetic→"injection" **0.95** vs on-manifold patch→"prompt" **0.45** despite the patch's **5× higher**
  presence (1.95 vs 0.33) — anomaly/coherence, anti-correlated with presence; the same patch read
  "injected" 0.73 by the 2AFC (internal inconsistency). Prompt-inference: paraphrase positive control
  0.996. Instrument valid (positive control PASS; comprehension 1.00).
- **S6 — layer-invariant.** Sweeping the injection layer L0→L56: 5a opposed picks-injected **< 0.5 at every
  layer** (global max **0.42** @ L24); **no source positive survives BH-FDR** (0/20); replicated on the
  held-out 46-concept set at L8/L40/L48 (opposed 0.28/0.39/0.26). The proposal's "early≈prompt /
  late-diverge" geometry **holds — but as salience/anomaly, not source** (§7).
- **S7 — cross-model general.** The negative generalizes to **Llama-3.3-70B** (different family + more
  capable) and **Qwen2.5-72B** (within-family scale-up): max opposed picks-injected **0.495 / 0.458**
  (primary order-pooled), none surviving BH-FDR + the CI-lower>0.55 bar; injection demonstrably effective;
  same non-source structure; detection output-presence-gated with a priming boost (Llama 1.66×, Qwen-72B
  3.0× on the det≥1 metric; cf. 32B's 3.7× on clear detection). **Honest Llama-specific caveats** (they
  scope how much weight Llama's leg carries, not the verdict): its diff-of-means directions are far **less
  orthogonal** than 32B's (max |cos| **0.88**; two pairs nearly collinear), so the decisive metrics are
  re-run on the near-orthogonal pairs — where the L24 opposed point estimate reaches **0.55** but with a
  CI **[0.39, 0.72]** spanning chance (does not clear the 0.55 bar); its **working** regime is largely
  **out-of-support** (garbles into repetition at lower perturbation, so we lead on the sub latent + the
  coherent band); its 2AFC **comprehension is 0.80** (≈30% of trials unparsed, vs Qwen-72B/32B at 1.00).
  Qwen-72B and 32B carry all three arms cleanly and concordantly; Llama corroborates the negative on 5a +
  B1, with a thinner anomaly arm. None of these properties of the **injection method** changes the verdict.
- **S8 — the representational verdict (§8).** An external linear probe finds no source decodable beyond
  re-reading-the-prompt + concept-agnostic graft/anomaly; in the balanced two-concept design, source ≡
  relative internal magnitude is **non-identifiable**; the deny-direction ablation fails.

---

## 6. The bounded positive, stated precisely

There **is** a real, weak signal — and it is decidedly **not** source-attribution. The model/residual
carries a weak, **prompt-text-dominated** **relative-internal-magnitude / anomaly** cue: sub-threshold, it
reads *which of two co-present concepts is internally more active*, concentrated in **affective/object**
concepts (emotion [n=8] **0.74**, object [n=7] **0.67**; concrete/abstract ≈ chance; **style inverted to
0.19** — the style injection systematically pushes the model to pick the *noun* option, so even the
clearest internal sensitivity is read through an output/entity prior, not as correct source/identity; two
tiny-n categories, positive [n=2] and persona [n=2], are also above chance but are not category-level
claims). The cue:

- is **dominated by prompt-text salience** (under opposed emphasis the model follows the ~1σ prompt over
  the 20–50σ injection at every layer × model);
- tracks the injected concept's **internal projection-z more than realized output-presence**, *weakly*
  (S6 §6C within-layer β_z = **+0.078 [0.001, 0.169]**, surviving partialling out the impending-output
  projection, vs β_presence ≈ −0.05) — **but the comparison β_z > β_presence is not significant**, the
  between-layer association is opposite, and the "magnitude" it tracks is **off-manifold anomaly
  magnitude** (z ≫ the natural 0.4σ). **This internal-z lean is primarily a 32B observation**: on Llama-70B
  and Qwen-72B the §6C dissociation does not clearly replicate (β_z ≈ β_presence ≈ 0, CIs include 0),
  consistent with its weakness;
- is **non-identifiable** from source by construction (an injection necessarily raises internal magnitude;
  §8).

So: a weak within-layer sensitivity to **relative internal (off-manifold) concept-magnitude / anomaly,
prompt-dominated — NOT a clean introspection positive, and NOT source.**

---

## 7. The reinterpreted geometric hypothesis

The proposal predicted "early-layer injection ≈ prompt route; later layers diverge, and the model can read
the divergence." The divergence **is** there and **does grow with depth** — but a direct measurement shows
it is **salience + anomaly, not source** (Figure 2):

- **Relative-salience 2AFC** is ≈ chance near the input (the near-input coincidence), **single-peaked at
  L40** (sub latent **0.742 [0.586, 0.875]**; held-out 46-concept set **0.707 [0.629, 0.783]** @ L40 vs ≈
  chance @ L8 0.518 / L48 0.529; an inverted-U, not a monotone climb), then falls.
- The on-manifold **patch** is read **"prompt"** at L0–L32 (says-injection 0.22–0.47 *despite* carrying
  higher output-presence than the synthetic) and **flips to "injection" at a sharp L40 onset** (held-out
  **0.996**) — an anomaly/manifold-status onset, not source.
- **The premise itself does not cleanly hold.** A direct representational measurement gives
  cos(injection-state, prompt-state) ≤ **0.34** (mid-stack max), **barely above the cross-concept floor
  0.26** — the injection-state is **not concept-specifically aligned** with the prompt-state — and the
  injection's concept content is far off the natural manifold (injection-site z ≈ 2.6–6.3σ vs the
  natural *unsteered* prompt-state's ~0.4σ — a final-state geometry measurement, distinct from the ~1σ
  internal projection of an emphasized prompt *mention* used in the 5a competition). Near
  the input the injection is not "prompt-equivalent"; it is simply **ineffective** (garbles before it
  injects). At all working layers it is an **off-manifold, louder** state — which is *why* the only thing
  the model reads off it is salience/anomaly.

![Figure 2 — the reinterpreted geometry: salience peak + anomaly onset + the premise that does not hold](../plots/final_fig2_salience_anomaly_geometry.png)

**Chain-of-thought recovers no source** (S6 §6D): letting the model deliberate before answering produces
**prompt-inference** ("I have not discussed X ⇒ it was introduced externally") at the **same high rate
(0.6–0.98) for a real injection, a content-free random vector, and no injection at all**; the CoT-2AFC ≈
the first-token latent.

---

## 8. The representational verdict (S8) — non-identifiability, not absence

The standing bound throughout S5–S7 was: *source information could still be present in the activations,
recoverable by an external probe but not self-reported.* Segment 8 settles this with an external **linear**
probe (Figure 4), in two phases.

**Phase 0 — one-concept matched-text probe.** Put the concept in the prompt for **all** conditions (so "c
in prompt" is matched and uninformative) and vary only the injection; held-out concepts; off-manifold
random + on-manifold neutral-graft + strict disjoint-distractor floors. Result: the probe **demonstrably
reads concept content** (patch-vs-neutral **~0.99** held-out) and off-manifoldness (synthetic-vs-prompt
~0.94), but **synthetic ≈ random** (off-manifold separability is concept-agnostic), and the
**prompt-vs-injection SOURCE boundary is concept-agnostic graft**: prompt-vs-patch = prompt-vs-neutral =
prompt-vs-distractor = **1.00**, and that boundary **transfers at 1.00** to both the content-free neutral
graft and the distractor graft. A hidden source signal would have made the prompt-vs-patch direction
separate concept-c grafts from concept-free grafts; it does not. The untried **deny-direction ABLATION
also fails**: projecting the report/deny axis out is inert (concept-sub detection stays **0.00**;
no-injection FPR stays **0.00**), and the additive form is a wholesale yes-bias on a high FPR floor (at
bias 1.0, no-injection FPR → 1.00 ≥ concept). The model's explicit "injection" verdict keys on
**prompt-inference** (a content-free neutral graft whose meta names an absent concept → "injection"
**1.00**) plus a weak, non-significant manifold-status lean (synthetic 0.63 vs patch 0.54, concept-clustered
CI **[−0.15, 0.32]** includes 0).

**Phase 1 — balanced two-concept probe** (both concepts in the prompt, inject one, probe reads which —
the representational analogue of the 5a test). Verdict = a **non-identifiability negative**:

1. **Non-identifiability (load-bearing, probe-independent).** The injected concept is **always** the
   more-internally-active one (its projection-z is ~14σ synthetic / ~4σ patch while the other co-present
   concept stays ~0 *even when text-emphasized*). A concept-aware proj-z **magnitude oracle** therefore
   decodes "which was injected" at **1.00** with no residual headroom for a separate source signal. You
   cannot construct a trial where source ≠ relative-internal-magnitude — *an injection that does not raise
   internal activation is not an injection.*
2. **The readout has power; the signal that exists is magnitude/anomaly, not source.** A held-out positive
   control (detect injection-vs-none) is **0.89–0.96** (the readout is far from dead). The held-out
   which-of-two **source probe** reaches
   **0.65–0.66 (linear) / 0.71 (MLP)** even in the cleanest output-presence-**matched** arm — a real
   above-chance signal — but it stays **below the concept-aware magnitude oracle at every non-degenerate
   synthetic cell** (source − oracle = −0.14 to −0.44) and, on the **pre-registered salience-matched
   subset**, is **≤ the proj-z predictor** (gap significantly negative, e.g. −0.26 [−0.34, −0.18] at L48).
   With output-presence matched, it is specifically the **internal projection-z** bounded positive (§6),
   not source.
3. **The on-manifold injection cannot even host the balanced design.** The full-context sequence patch is
   all-or-nothing: grafting concept c **erases** the other co-present concept (its output-presence → 0.06,
   text-emphasis → 0.00). So the only both-concepts-preserving injection is the off-manifold synthetic one
   — already the best case for a positive.

![Figure 4 — the representational verdict: source < magnitude oracle (balanced) + concept-agnostic graft boundary (matched-text)](../plots/final_fig4_representational_probe.png)

**Honesty note (a self-correction we preserve).** Two measurement-validity reviews caught that an initial
"airtight" framing rested on two artifacts; we do **not** cite them as evidence: **(a)** the **opposed-arm
0.50** is *degenerate by construction* (the opposed context depends only on (emphasized, injected), so
pooling option order puts the byte-identical residual under both labels — forced to 0.50, carrying no
information); **(b)** the **salience-probe gap ≈ 0** is *definitional* where the z-separation is large
(injected ≡ higher-proj-z). The representational negative rests instead on: the source probe staying
**below the concept-aware proj-z magnitude oracle** at every non-degenerate cell + on the salience-matched
subset, the **non-identifiability argument**, the **positive control**, the **patch erasure**, and
**Phase 0**.

**The structural inversion (the result as a near-theorem, not a failure).** Notice what regime *is*
decodable: an **off-manifold** synthetic injection separates from the prompt — but that separation is
concept-agnostic **anomaly** (synthetic ≈ random), not source; and the **on-manifold** injection that would
carry a clean source contrast is *prompt-like by construction* and (in the balanced design)
non-identifiable from relative magnitude. So *the only regime in which "source" is decodable is the one in
which it reduces to anomaly / magnitude detection* — which is exactly why a fair source readout has no
operating point. This is a structural / non-identifiability statement about the injection paradigm and
these readouts, **not** a metaphysical claim that source is unrepresentable in principle.

---

## 9. What each result licenses + the comprehensive bounds

**What the program licenses.** A fair, pre-registered, cross-model, layer-invariant, behaviorally +
representationally converging conclusion: *Qwen2.5-32B/72B and Llama-3.3-70B do not exhibit
source-attribution introspection above the salience / anomaly / output-reading / prompt-inference floors,
under the injection methods, layers, and readouts tested* — with the injection demonstrably effective and
the four confounds explicitly controlled. Plus a precisely-characterized bounded relative-salience positive
(§6).

**The comprehensive, standing bounds** (carried forward; the negative is *bounded*, not metaphysical):

- **Model / method / readout-specific.** Three open models (Qwen2.5-32B/72B, Llama-3.3-70B); diff-of-means
  + position-for-position sequence-patch injection; verbal-2AFC / first-token-latent / CoT readouts. The
  cross-model leg is **strongest on Qwen-72B and 32B** (all arms clean and concordant); **Llama-70B is a
  weaker instrument** — less-orthogonal diff-of-means directions (max |cos| 0.88), a largely
  out-of-support working regime, 2AFC comprehension 0.80 (~30% unparsed), and a thinner anomaly arm — so
  its null leans on 5a + B1 and carries less weight than the Qwen models'.
- **70–72B is below frontier / closed Claude.** The literature's strongest introspection was on *closed,
  non-injectable* Claude. We rule out a *capability-within-the-open-model-range* explanation, **not** a
  frontier-capability positive.
- **Linear / single-MLP probe ≠ all probes.** Source as **relational binding** could live in attention/QK
  circuits or a non-linear readout outside a single-token residual probe; an MLP probe (0.71) was also
  below the oracle, but this does not exhaust all probes or post-training.
- **Off-manifold / graft non-identifiability.** A coherent, presence-matched, **on-manifold** injected
  state grafted into context is **prompt-like by construction** (it *is* real conversational residuals), so
  "which source produced this state" may not be recoverable from the state. The on-manifold balanced design
  is **not constructible with the full-context sequence-patch primitive tested** (the patch erases the
  other concept) — sub-span / interpolated grafts are an untried future direction.
- **By-construction non-identifiability.** Injection necessarily raises internal magnitude, so within the
  injection paradigm "source" and "relative internal magnitude" cannot be dissociated — a positive in the
  balanced design was, in retrospect, unreachable by construction.
- **The autoregressive output-reading limit.** The residual stream partly *is* the impending output, so a
  pre-first-token latent readout severs but does not perfectly eliminate the confound; and "consistent with
  output-reading" is not strictly proven against an internal-reportability-threshold account (the
  decisive output-clamping cell leaks because most detectors paraphrase around a literal-token mask).
- **Injection dose / position / dynamics, and concept breadth.** We matched strength at the tested sub /
  working operating points and swept the injection layer (L0→L56), but did not finely sweep a narrow
  strength window, alternative injected positions/spans, or **dynamic / temporal (layer-by-layer build-up)**
  signatures of the prompt route as a candidate introspective cue (the injection is a static offset; a
  prompt-borne concept has a build-up trajectory). And the bounded positive is **affective/object-concept-
  concentrated** — abstract / self-referential / task-state concepts could behave differently. These are
  open directions, not established negatives.

---

## 10. Relation to prior work

The project was framed around four prior results; for each, what our result does to it:

- **Anthropic, "Signs of introspection in LLMs"** (~20% detection on closed Claude). We **replicate weak
  detection on open models** (S2: clear detection 0.104 primed at 0 FPR) but **qualify** it sharply: it is
  **output-dependent** — it collapses to the FPR floor (≈0) sub-threshold (S3 gate FAIL on
  output-independence) — and it does **not** isolate **source-attribution** (S4–S8). What looked like
  "noticing an injected thought before it biased the output" is, on these open models, gated by output
  bias.
- **"Latent Introspection" (Qwen-32B).** The **priming boost replicates** (~3.7× on 32B clear detection;
  ~3.0× on Qwen-72B and ~1.66× on Llama-70B on the inclusive det≥1 metric) but **does not** yield genuine
  sub-threshold detection; the logit-lens "latent detection"
  is **output-presence-gated** (first-token P(Yes) ≈ 0 sub-threshold). We **qualify** the latent-detection
  claim: the latent signal we can find is present only where output is concurrently biased.
- **"Mechanisms of Introspective Awareness" (two-stage evidence-carrier → deny-gate circuit).** We
  **operationalize and test** the specific lever it predicts — the **deny-direction ABLATION** — and it
  **fails** (projection-out inert; additive = a wholesale yes-bias). On these models the data do **not**
  support a recoverable suppressed-detection signal behind a deny gate (scoped to this diff-of-means
  direction; deeper circuit tracing is out of scope).
- **"Dissociating Direct Access from Inference."** Our prompt-vs-activation benchmark **operationalizes and
  sharpens** this distinction by directly pitting concept-tokens-in-prompt against a concept-vector-in-
  activations. We find the apparent discrimination **is inference** (salience / anomaly / prompt-reading),
  with **no demonstrated direct access** to the source of an internal state.

**Bounding the novelty claim honestly.** What is genuinely **new**: the fair, pre-registered, cross-model,
de-confounded **negative** to a sharp source-attribution question, plus the **methodology** (matched
salience load-bearing on output-presence; mandatory content-free + on-manifold-patch controls; held-out
concepts; first-token-latent output-severing; the balanced two-concept source probe + the
non-identifiability argument; outcome-licensing + MC-aware criteria). What merely **replicates** prior
observations: weak primed detection (Anthropic), the priming boost (Latent Introspection), the additive
yes-bias of a detect-direction (Mechanisms).

---

## 11. Future work (what would change or extend the conclusion)

- **Frontier + closed, introspection-tuned models** (the literature's strongest effect was on closed
  Claude; not injectable here).
- **On-manifold sub-span / interpolated grafts** — a both-concepts-preserving on-manifold injection that
  the full-context sequence patch cannot construct (it erases the host); the single most direct way to
  attack the off-manifold / by-construction non-identifiability bound.
- **Non-linear / relational / attention-circuit probes**, and **trained / fine-tuned-readout** probes (the
  "was this injected?" variable is OOD for the base model).
- **Alternative injection methods** beyond diff-of-means; **introspection-specific fine-tuning**.

---

## 12. Contribution and what it means for the introspection literature

The project's central value is a **rigorously-established, fair, cross-model NEGATIVE** plus the
**methodology** that makes it trustworthy. Stated at calibrated strength:

- **Strong enough.** It is a clean, fair, **pre-registered**, **cross-model** (two families + a scale-up +
  increased capability), **layer-invariant** (L0→L56), **behaviorally + representationally converging**
  result, with the injection **demonstrably effective** and the four confounds **explicitly controlled**.
  The apparent "introspection" in this paradigm reduces to relative salience, off-manifold anomaly,
  output-reading, and prompt-inference. The likely mechanism is **structural** (the autoregressive
  residual-is-output confound + the off-manifold injection), not a 32B-specific capability gap.
- **Bounded enough.** It is **not** "models cannot introspect." It is a **non-identifiability** negative,
  specific to these models / injection methods / readouts, with the linear/relational/off-manifold/
  by-construction and below-frontier bounds standing. A coherent on-manifold injected state is prompt-like
  by construction, so the cleanest fair comparison is partly non-identifiable; and "which is injected" is
  non-identifiable from "which is more internally active."

For the introspection literature this is a cautionary, constructive result: **before crediting an
introspection report, control for salience, off-manifold anomaly, output-reading, and prompt-inference, and
match on downstream output-presence** — because on three open models, once those are controlled, the
apparent source-introspection signal disappears, while the instruments retain the power to have found it.

---

## Reproducibility, cost, figures, and files

- **Reproducibility.** All Modal generations and grader calls are cached; the **analysis + grading
  pipeline reproduces from cache** (`--assert-cached`). The headline-numbers audit (`results/
  final_numbers_audit.md`) maps every headline claim to its committed `results/*.json` + value, and
  documents the one benign caveat (GPU-generation `--assert-cached` re-resolves a new Modal container
  `image_id`, which is part of the cache key, so it misses on the image-identity field only — the
  model-side code hash is unchanged, the cached generation **outputs** are the committed `results/*.jsonl`,
  and the grading layer is fully cache-clean; **no headline number is affected**).
- **Total project cost** (consolidated from `total_cost.jsonl`): **≈ $1,066** (Modal GPU + Anthropic
  grading + reviews), across S0–S9.
- **Figures** (all assembled from committed summary JSONs by `plot_final.py`; per-segment plots retained):
  Figure 1 `plots/final_fig1_headline_crossmodel.png`, Figure 2 `plots/final_fig2_salience_anomaly_geometry.png`,
  Figure 3 `plots/final_fig3_falsifiability_power.png`, Figure 4 `plots/final_fig4_representational_probe.png`.
- **Where the detail lives.** Per-segment write-ups `writeups/write_up_s0..s8*.md`; the design doc
  `writeups/benchmark_design_s4.md`; the gate `results/replication_gate.md`; pre-registrations
  `writeups/prereg_s*.md`; the consolidated phase log `writeups/write_up.md`; reproduction instructions
  `writeups/continuation_context.md`.

report rounds, the committed value is shown. **Discrepancies found vs prior phase write-ups are flagged
with ⚠ and the report uses the committed value.**

## ⚠ Discrepancies found vs prior write-ups (report uses the committed value)

1. **S6 32B opposed global max.** `write_up_s6_layer_sweep.md` says "max 0.41 at L40" in its headline
   prose, but the committed `s6_summary.json` (`multiple_comparison_source_fdr.max_picks_injected_any_layer`
   and `B3_source_emphasis_by_layer.working`) shows the global max is **0.42 at L24** (L40 working = 0.406).
   The top-level `write_up.md` already states "global max 0.42 @L24". **The final report uses 0.42 @ L24.**
   (Both are < 0.5; the negative is unchanged.)
2. **S5 5a opposed CI.** `write_up_s5_discrimination_full.md` cites "0.39 [0.35, 0.46]"; the committed
   `s5_summary.json` gives **0.39 [0.34, 0.44]** (Wilson) / **[0.33, 0.45]** (clustered). **The final
   report uses the committed CIs.**

3. **"Below chance at every layer × model" — one non-significant secondary-analysis exception (Llama).**
   `s7_summary_llama70b.json` → `robustness_near_orthogonal_pairs.opposed_working_picks_injected.24` =
   **0.5486** (CI **[0.389, 0.722]**, spanning chance) — the near-orthogonal-pairs robustness re-run that
   drops Llama's shared-subspace-contaminated pairs. **The final report now scopes the universal phrasing
   to the "primary order-pooled analysis" and explicitly notes this 0.55 (n.s., does not clear the
   CI-lower>0.55 bar) Llama secondary cell** in §2 / §5 / §9, plus Llama's less-orthogonal directions (max
   |cos| 0.88), out-of-support working regime, and 0.80 comprehension.

No discrepancy affects any qualitative conclusion (every *primary* opposed source point estimate is < 0.5;
the single secondary Llama cell at 0.55 is non-significant and fails the pre-registered source bar).

---

## Reproducibility verification (`--assert-cached`)

The production pipeline reruns from cache at the layers that matter for the headline numbers:

- **Analysis / plotting layer** (`analyze_*.py`, `plot_*.py`, `plot_final.py`): regenerates every summary
  JSON and figure from the committed `results/*.jsonl`/`*.json` with **no API calls** — verified for
  `analyze_s5`, `analyze_s6`, `analyze_s7`, `analyze_s8_probe --design matched_text`, `analyze_s8b_probe`,
  and `plot_final`.
- **Grading layer** (the paid Opus layer; `run_grade_*.py --assert-cached`): **fully cache-clean — $0.00**
  — verified across segments (`run_grade_salience` on s5/s7 steerscans, `run_grade_coherence` on s6,
  `run_grade_introspect` on s8 deny, `run_grade_s8b_present`). This confirms the committed `gen_*.jsonl`
  are intact and every grader call reproduces from cache.
- **GPU-generation layer** (`run_*.py --assert-cached`): the generation **outputs** are materialized as the
  committed `results/gen_*.jsonl`; re-running `--assert-cached` now **misses on the `image_id` field only**.
  The Modal cache key is `{model_id, method, kwargs, image_id, code_hash, sample_idx, this_call_uuid}`
  (`modal_runner.py`), where `image_id = image.object_id`. Entering `app.run()` re-resolved a **new**
  container image build id (`im-PhHQoiIREvcGc5jwW7Alra`) that differs from the id baked into the cached
  entries — an environment-drift artifact in the **container-image identity**, not a change in the
  model-side code (`code_hash` = `88851b869ea239d5`, unchanged), the inputs, or the results. No
  `steering_modal.py` edit was made (which would change `code_hash` and invalidate all Modal caches), and
  no committed S0–S8 GPU result was re-run.

**Net:** every headline number is computed by the cache-clean analysis layer from the committed
`gen_*.jsonl` / `graded_*.jsonl`, and the paid grading layer is fully `--assert-cached` clean. The
GPU-generation `image_id` miss does **not** affect any headline number (the outputs are committed and
unchanged); it is the project's documented "bit-exact GPU reproduction is not the norm; `--assert-cached`
reproduces the cached outputs" caveat, here at the level of Modal's image-object identity.
